question: What are the main differences between Data Definition Language (DDL) and Data Manipulation Language (DML)? Provide an example command for each.
expectedPoints: DDL is used to define and modify database structure (e.g., CREATE, ALTER, DROP)
DML is used to manipulate data within the database objects (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE)
DDL commands are auto-committed and cannot be rolled back, while DML commands can be rolled back

question: Describe the three-schema architecture of a DBMS and explain the purpose of each level.
expectedPoints: Internal Level: Describes the physical storage structure and access paths (low-level, closest to hardware)
Conceptual Level: Describes the entire database structure and constraints for all users (community view)
External Level: Describes part of the database for a particular group of users (user views)
Purpose: Provides data independence by separating user applications from the physical database

question: Compare and contrast the roles of a Database Administrator (DBA) and a Database Designer. Explain how their responsibilities interact throughout the database lifecycle, including the tools and languages each would primarily use.
expectedPoints: Database Designer: Focuses on logical and physical design; uses conceptual modeling tools (ER diagrams) and DDL to implement the design
Database Administrator: Manages technical implementation, security, performance, and maintenance; uses DCL, performance tuning tools, and backup/recovery utilities
Interaction: Designer creates the structure, DBA implements and maintains it; both work together during initial design and schema evolution
Lifecycle involvement: Designer primarily in requirements analysis and design phases; DBA involved from implementation through operation and maintenance
Additional considerations: Discuss how both roles contribute to data integrity, security, and performance optimization
